# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Define the temporary file path
TEMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"

# Ensure the temporary file is removed when the script exits, even on error
trap "rm -f \"$TEMP_FILE\"" EXIT

echo "Script started. Temporary file: $TEMP_FILE"

# --- Phase 1: Write initial data to the temporary file ---
echo "Writing initial data to $TEMP_FILE..."
echo "Line 1: Raw data" > "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Line 2: More raw data" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Line 3: Yet another line" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Initial content of $TEMP_FILE:"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"
echo

# --- Phase 2: Process data from the temporary file and write results back ---
echo "Processing data from $TEMP_FILE and writing results back to it..."
# Read the content, transform it (e.g., convert to uppercase and add a prefix), then overwrite the file
PROCESSED_CONTENT=$(cat "$TEMP_FILE" | awk '{print "PROCESSED: " toupper($0)}')
echo "$PROCESSED_CONTENT" > "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Content of $TEMP_FILE after first processing:"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"
echo

# --- Phase 3: Further processing using the same temporary file ---
echo "Performing further processing on $TEMP_FILE..."
# Let's add a header and footer to the file
CURRENT_CONTENT=$(cat "$TEMP_FILE")
echo "--- HEADER ---" > "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "$CURRENT_CONTENT" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "--- FOOTER ---" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Content of $TEMP_FILE after second processing:"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"
echo

# --- Phase 4: Read final results from the temporary file ---
echo "Reading final results from $TEMP_FILE:"
FINAL_RESULTS=$(cat "$TEMP_FILE")
echo "--- FINAL RESULTS ---"
echo "$FINAL_RESULTS"
echo "---------------------"
echo

echo "Script finished. Temporary file will be removed."
